enrichment.short_description: Web-based and desktop PDF editor for merging, splitting, editing, signing, converting, and compressing PDF files.
enrichment.starting_price: $7.50/month (Web Monthly plan; a one-time 7-day Web Week Pass and an annual Desktop+Web plan are also offered)

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Sejda is a PDF editing and management platform that has been operating since 2010, built around a simple premise: most people need to merge, split, edit, or convert a PDF only occasionally, and shouldn't have to install heavyweight desktop software to do it. The company, based in Amsterdam, Netherlands, runs a browser-based service at sejda.com alongside an optional desktop application for Mac, Windows, and Linux.

The product covers more than 30 discrete PDF tools, spanning document assembly (merge, split, extract, delete, reorder pages), content editing (add text, images, links, shapes, and signatures), format conversion (PDF to and from Word, Excel, PowerPoint, JPG, and HTML), and document security (password protection, encryption, watermarking, and redaction). OCR is also built in, letting scanned or image-based PDFs be searched and edited.

Sejda operates on a freemium model: a no-signup-required free tier handles light, occasional use within daily task and file-size limits, while paid Web and Desktop+Web plans remove those limits and add features like multi-file batch processing and longer processing time per task. This structure has made it a widely used utility for individuals and small teams handling routine PDF work rather than large-scale document management.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sejda PDF Is For
paragraphs: Sejda suits freelancers, students, and small-business owners who need to handle PDFs occasionally but don't want to buy or learn a full desktop suite. Tasks like filling out a form, merging a handful of scanned receipts, or converting a contract to Word are exactly the kind of one-off jobs the free tier is designed to cover without requiring an account.

It also works well for administrative and office staff who process moderate volumes of paperwork, such as HR documents, invoices, or client contracts, and want a single web tool that covers merging, editing, e-signatures, and conversion rather than juggling several single-purpose apps. Because file uploads are processed and then deleted from Sejda's servers, it can be a reasonable choice for anyone who wants to avoid installing unfamiliar software on a work machine.

Users who occasionally need OCR to make an old scanned document searchable, or who need to quickly redact or password-protect a sensitive file before sending it, are also a good fit. The paid plans extend the same simple workflow to slightly heavier, recurring use without introducing the complexity of enterprise document-management software.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Large organizations that need a full document-management or workflow-automation platform, with audit trails, granular permissions, and deep integrations into content management systems, will likely find Sejda too lightweight. It is built as a focused PDF utility, not a document lifecycle or collaboration suite.

Teams that depend on programmatic access, such as automating PDF generation or conversion from within their own applications, may also be limited, since Sejda's public offering is centered on interactive web and desktop use rather than a developer API. Businesses whose workflows depend on mobile-first PDF editing should note that Sejda does not offer dedicated iOS or Android apps, relying instead on a browser or desktop client.

Heavy daily users who process large volumes of confidential PDFs may also want to weigh the free tier's daily task and page limits against a paid subscription early, since the free plan is intentionally scoped for occasional rather than continuous use.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At its core, Sejda offers document assembly tools: merging multiple PDFs and images into a single file, splitting a document by page ranges, bookmarks, size, or text, and extracting or deleting specific pages. Its editor allows users to add or modify text, images, links, and shapes directly on a PDF page, plus fill out forms and add signatures for lightweight sign-off workflows.

Conversion is a major part of the toolset, with support for turning PDFs into Word, Excel, PowerPoint, JPG, and plain text, and converting Word, JPG, or HTML files into PDF. Compression tools reduce file size for easier sharing or storage, and OCR processing (available up to a page limit that scales with the plan) converts scanned or image-based pages into searchable, selectable text.

Security and cleanup features round out the platform: adding or removing passwords and permissions, applying watermarks, flattening forms, redacting sensitive content, and deskewing scanned pages. The optional desktop application mirrors much of this functionality for offline use on Mac, Windows, and Linux, which matters for users who prefer not to upload sensitive files to a web server at all.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Sejda PDF Experience
paragraphs: The day-to-day experience of using Sejda is built around speed and minimal friction. Most tools are reachable directly from the homepage without an account, and a typical task (uploading a file, making an edit, and downloading the result) takes only a few clicks. The interface stays close to a single-purpose design, showing only the controls relevant to the tool in use rather than a crowded, all-in-one dashboard.

Files uploaded to the web app are processed on Sejda's servers over an encrypted connection and are stated to be deleted after processing, which is a meaningful consideration for anyone editing sensitive contracts, forms, or personal documents. Users who want to avoid any server upload at all can instead run the desktop application locally, trading a small amount of convenience for offline processing.

Where the experience can feel more constrained is around the free tier's daily task and file-size limits, which are enforced clearly rather than hidden, nudging frequent users toward a paid plan. Overall, the experience reads as a pragmatic, no-nonsense utility rather than a polished productivity suite, which fits its positioning as a fast way to solve a specific PDF problem rather than a place to manage a document library long-term.

features.title: Merge PDFs
features.description: Combine multiple PDF files and images into a single document with control over page order.

features.title: Split and Extract
features.description: Split a PDF by page ranges, bookmarks, file size, or text, or extract and delete specific pages.

features.title: PDF Editor
features.description: Add or edit text, images, links, shapes, and whiteout directly on existing PDF pages.

features.title: Fill and Sign
features.description: Fill out PDF forms and add signatures for lightweight approval and sign-off workflows.

features.title: Format Conversion
features.description: Convert PDFs to Word, Excel, PowerPoint, JPG, and text, and convert Word, JPG, or HTML files into PDF.

features.title: Compression
features.description: Reduce PDF file size for easier sharing and storage while aiming to preserve visual quality.

features.title: OCR
features.description: Turn scanned or image-based PDF pages into searchable, selectable, and editable text.

features.title: Document Security
features.description: Add or remove passwords and permissions, apply watermarks, redact content, and flatten forms.

features.title: Desktop Application
features.description: An offline Mac, Windows, and Linux app for processing PDFs without uploading files to a server.

pros: Free tier requires no account signup for basic, occasional PDF tasks
Broad toolset of 30-plus features covers most common PDF needs in one place
Uploaded files are stated to be deleted from servers after processing
Optional offline desktop app for Mac, Windows, and Linux
Simple, uncluttered interface that is easy to pick up without training
cons: Free plan enforces daily task, file size, and page limits
No dedicated iOS or Android mobile apps
Limited collaboration or team-workflow features compared to full document-management platforms
No public developer API for programmatic PDF processing
Regular or high-volume use effectively requires a paid subscription

faqs.question: Is Sejda PDF free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sejda offers a free tier that supports a limited number of tasks per day along with file-size and page limits, and it does not require creating an account. Paid Web and Desktop+Web plans remove these limits.

faqs.question: Do I need to create an account to use Sejda?
faqs.answer: No, most core tools on the free tier can be used directly from the website without signing up. An account becomes relevant when subscribing to a paid plan.

faqs.question: Is it safe to upload sensitive PDFs to Sejda?
faqs.answer: Sejda states that uploaded files are transferred over an encrypted connection and deleted from its servers after processing. Users who prefer not to upload files at all can use the offline desktop application instead.

faqs.question: Does Sejda have a desktop application?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sejda offers a desktop app compatible with Mac, Windows, and Linux that processes files locally, which is included with the Desktop+Web annual plan.

faqs.question: Can Sejda edit scanned or image-based PDFs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sejda includes OCR functionality that converts scanned or image-based PDF pages into searchable and editable text, with the number of OCR pages depending on the plan.

use_cases.title: Filling and signing contracts
use_cases.description: Quickly fill out PDF forms and add a signature to agreements or contracts without printing and scanning.

use_cases.title: Converting scanned documents to editable text
use_cases.description: Use OCR to turn scanned or photographed pages into searchable, editable text for reuse or citation.

use_cases.title: Organizing multi-file document sets
use_cases.description: Merge, split, and reorder large batches of PDFs and scanned files into a single organized document.

use_cases.title: Securing sensitive documents
use_cases.description: Password-protect, redact, or watermark confidential files such as HR records or legal documents before sharing.

features: 7 days of access to Sejda Web
Unlimited documents, no page or hourly limits
Unlimited documents, no page or hourly limits
Up to 21 minutes processing time per task
A year of access to Sejda Desktop
Unlimited documents, no page or hourly limits

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sejda PDF review: pricing, features, and who it's for. See how this web and desktop PDF editor handles merging, editing, converting, and signing files.

enrichment.short_description: Sellfy is an e-commerce platform for creators to sell digital products, subscriptions, and print-on-demand merch with built-in marketing tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Sellfy is an e-commerce platform built for independent creators who want to sell digital products, physical merchandise, and subscriptions without stitching together a website, a payment processor, and a fulfillment partner on their own. Founded in 2011 and headquartered in Riga, Latvia, the company has grown from a simple digital-download storefront into a broader toolkit that also covers print-on-demand merch and recurring memberships, positioning itself as a lighter, creator-focused alternative to larger commerce platforms such as Shopify.

At its core, Sellfy lets sellers upload files such as ebooks, music, presets, templates, or software, set a price, and generate a hosted store or embeddable buy button in minutes. Beyond downloads, it layers in print-on-demand apparel and accessories that are produced and shipped by partner facilities when an order comes in, plus subscription billing for creators who want to charge on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis for ongoing access to content. The goal across all of these product types is to let a single creator run a storefront without needing a developer, a warehouse, or a separate fulfillment vendor.

The platform runs on tiered monthly or annual subscription pricing rather than a percentage-based transaction fee, which Sellfy markets as a 0% transaction-fee model across every plan. There is no permanent free plan, but a 14-day free trial lets prospective sellers test the storefront builder, marketing tools, and checkout flow before committing to a paid tier, and a 30-day money-back guarantee softens the risk of a first upgrade.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sellfy Is For
paragraphs: Sellfy fits solo creators and small teams best: authors and course creators selling ebooks or guides, designers selling templates and presets, musicians selling tracks or sample packs, and educators packaging downloadable resources. These sellers typically want a store live quickly, without hiring a developer, and value having digital delivery, licensing, and file protection handled for them out of the box rather than configured from scratch.

It also suits creators who want to diversify beyond digital files into physical merchandise without holding inventory. Because print-on-demand production and shipping are handled by Sellfy's fulfillment network, a YouTuber, artist, or podcaster can add branded apparel or accessories to an existing digital storefront with minimal added operational work, and without pre-purchasing stock or managing a warehouse.

Finally, Sellfy appeals to creators building recurring revenue through memberships or subscription content, and to anyone who wants their marketing (email campaigns, discount codes, upsells) managed inside the same dashboard as their storefront rather than through a separate suite of disconnected tools. Sellers just getting started, or those consolidating several smaller tools into one account, tend to get the most value from this bundled approach.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Larger retailers and multi-channel sellers who need deep inventory management, multi-warehouse logistics, or native integrations with marketplaces like Amazon or Etsy will likely find Sellfy too limited. Its feature set is intentionally narrow compared to full commerce platforms, so complex catalogs or high-SKU physical retail operations are not its strength, and teams outgrowing a simple product catalog should look elsewhere.

Businesses that need extensive customization, such as a fully headless storefront, custom checkout logic, or deep API-driven workflows, may also outgrow Sellfy quickly, since its store builder favors simplicity and speed over granular developer control. Teams with in-house engineering resources who want full control over the buying experience will likely hit the platform's limits sooner than solo creators.

High-volume sellers should also weigh the plan-based annual sales caps built into Sellfy's pricing tiers. Once a store consistently exceeds the ceiling on its current plan, it must upgrade or contact Sellfy for custom pricing, which can make cost less predictable for fast-scaling businesses than a flat percentage-fee model that simply scales with revenue automatically.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: Sellfy's storefront builder lets sellers customize colors, fonts, and layout without writing code, and stores can also be embedded as buy buttons or product widgets on an existing website or blog. Digital file delivery includes secure hosting, download limits, and support for large file sizes that scale with the chosen plan, so the underlying infrastructure grows alongside the seller's catalog.

Print-on-demand is built directly into the same dashboard, covering apparel and accessories that are produced and shipped by partner facilities when a customer places an order, so sellers don't manage physical inventory. Subscription billing lets creators charge recurring fees for access to content or memberships, and built-in email marketing supports newsletters, product announcements, and cart-abandonment recovery on higher-tier plans.

Additional marketing tools include discount codes, product upselling, and an affiliate marketing option on the Business and Premium plans, while checkout supports both PayPal and Stripe with no added transaction fee from Sellfy itself. Basic store analytics round out the toolkit so sellers can track sales, orders, and customer activity from the same dashboard used to manage products and campaigns.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Sellfy Experience
paragraphs: Setting up a Sellfy store is generally fast: creators pick a plan, upload products, apply a storefront theme, and connect a payment processor without needing technical setup. This straightforward onboarding is one of the platform's most consistently cited strengths among creators comparing it to heavier e-commerce systems that require more configuration before a first sale can happen.

Day-to-day store management happens through a single dashboard covering products, orders, marketing campaigns, and basic analytics, and the account dashboard itself is available in English or Spanish. The customer-facing storefront and purchase emails support a wider set of languages, including French, German, Italian, Portuguese, and Russian, or can switch automatically based on a shopper's browser settings, which matters for creators selling to an international audience.

Support is delivered primarily through email and a self-serve help center, with priority support and store or product migration assistance reserved for the higher-tier Premium plan. Overall, the experience favors sellers who want a dependable, no-frills tool for turning digital and print-on-demand products into recurring income rather than a highly configurable enterprise commerce platform, and reviewers generally describe the learning curve as short.

features.title: No-code storefront builder
features.description: Customize colors, fonts, and layout to launch a hosted store or embeddable buy button without writing code.

features.title: Digital product delivery
features.description: Securely host and deliver ebooks, music, software, presets, and other downloadable files with configurable limits.

features.title: Print-on-demand merchandise
features.description: Sell apparel and accessories that are produced and shipped by partner fulfillment facilities per order, with no inventory to manage.

features.title: Subscription billing
features.description: Charge customers on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis for ongoing access to digital content or memberships.

features.title: Built-in email marketing
features.description: Send newsletters and product updates to your customer list directly from the Sellfy dashboard.

features.title: Cart abandonment recovery
features.description: Automatically follow up with shoppers who leave items in their cart, available on Business plan and above.

features.title: Discount codes and upsells
features.description: Create promo codes and product upsell offers at checkout to increase average order value.

features.title: Affiliate marketing
features.description: Let affiliates promote products and earn commissions, available on Business and Premium plans.

features.title: Product embeds
features.description: Embed individual product buy buttons or widgets on an existing website or blog outside the Sellfy storefront.

features.title: PayPal and Stripe checkout with 0% transaction fees
features.description: Accept payments through PayPal or Stripe without Sellfy taking an additional cut of each sale.

pros: No transaction fees on top of the subscription plan price
Combines digital downloads, subscriptions, and print-on-demand merch in one dashboard
Simple, no-code storefront setup that creators can launch quickly
Built-in email marketing, discount codes, and upsell tools without extra add-ons
14-day free trial and a 30-day money-back guarantee for new upgrades
cons: No permanent free plan for sellers who want to start at no cost
Annual sales caps per plan mean high-growth stores must upgrade or negotiate custom pricing
Account dashboard is only available in English or Spanish, unlike the wider storefront language options
Less catalog depth and third-party integration breadth than larger e-commerce platforms
Cart abandonment emails and affiliate marketing are locked behind the Business plan or higher

faqs.question: Does Sellfy offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Sellfy does not currently offer a permanent free plan. It offers a 14-day free trial so sellers can test the platform before choosing a paid plan.

faqs.question: Does Sellfy charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: Sellfy advertises 0% transaction fees across all of its plans; sellers only pay their monthly or annual subscription price plus standard fees from their payment processor, such as PayPal or Stripe.

faqs.question: Can I sell physical products on Sellfy, or only digital files?
faqs.answer: Both. Sellfy supports digital downloads and subscriptions as well as print-on-demand physical merchandise like apparel and accessories, which are produced and shipped by partner fulfillment facilities.

faqs.question: What happens if my store's sales exceed my plan's annual limit?
faqs.answer: Each Sellfy plan (Starter, Business, Premium) includes an annual sales cap. Sellers who consistently exceed that cap need to upgrade to a higher plan, and stores exceeding the top Premium threshold can contact Sellfy about custom pricing.

faqs.question: What languages does Sellfy support?
faqs.answer: The customer-facing storefront and purchase emails can be set to English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German, Italian, or Russian, or set to adjust automatically based on the customer's browser. The seller account dashboard itself is available in English or Spanish only.

use_cases.title: Selling ebooks, guides, and courses
use_cases.description: Authors and educators upload downloadable content, set pricing, and deliver files securely through a hosted store or embedded buy button.
use_cases.audience: Independent authors, coaches, and course creators

use_cases.title: Print-on-demand merch for creators
use_cases.description: Creators add branded apparel and accessories to their store without holding inventory, since orders are produced and shipped by fulfillment partners.
use_cases.audience: YouTubers, artists, and podcasters building a merch line

use_cases.title: Selling music and digital audio
use_cases.description: Musicians and producers sell tracks, albums, or sample packs directly to fans with secure file delivery and licensing controls.

use_cases.title: Membership and subscription content
use_cases.description: Creators charge recurring fees for ongoing access to digital content or communities, using Sellfy's subscription billing tools.

features: Unlimited digital, subscription, and print-on-demand products

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sellfy review: pricing, features, pros and cons of the e-commerce platform for selling digital products, subscriptions, and print-on-demand merch.
